decisions.title: AI interpretation run creates background task with notification
decisions.detail: Each AI interpretation run will create one task that executes in the background within the task list, similar to the CC platform. A notification will be sent upon its completion.

evidence.quote: Each AI interpretation run creates one task, which runs in the background in the task list (similar to how the CC platform operates); a notification is sent when it completes

decisions.title: Notification mechanism details for AI interpretation tasks are pending confirmation
decisions.detail: The specific details of the notification mechanism for AI interpretation task completion require further confirmation during the requirements interview.

evidence.quote: Notification mechanism details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview

decisions.title: Two image sources for AI interpretation
decisions.detail: Imagery for AI interpretation will come from two sources: a one-time, large-scale nationwide batch provided by Vendor A, or individual images uploaded by the user.

evidence.quote: Two image sources: a one-time large-scale nationwide batch of imagery obtained from Vendor A, or individual images uploaded by the user

decisions.title: User confirmation and monitoring decision for suspected points
decisions.detail: After AI interpretation is complete and a list of suspected points is generated, users will be able to confirm (yes/no) these points and decide whether to place them under watch or monitoring.

evidence.quote: After interpretation completes, a list is generated; the user can confirm suspected points (yes/no) and decide whether to place them under watch/monitoring

decisions.title: Monitoring requires reason and polygon upload
decisions.detail: When placing a suspected point under monitoring, users must provide a reason and upload a corresponding polygon. Large-area polygons are acceptable, but overly small areas will be automatically filtered out.

evidence.quote: When placing a point under monitoring, the reason must be filled in and a polygon uploaded (large-area polygons are valid; overly small areas are automatically filtered out)

decisions.title: Duplicate image upload prevention
decisions.detail: Images must not be uploaded more than once due to their large file size, necessitating a duplicate-prevention mechanism.

evidence.quote: Images must not be uploaded more than once (image files are large, so a duplicate-prevention mechanism is needed)

decisions.title: Manual annotation for two-period image comparison
decisions.detail: The two-period image comparison feature will no longer source images from the image library; instead, users will annotate these manually.

evidence.quote: Two-period image comparison will no longer pull from the image library — the user will annotate this manually instead

decisions.title: On-site workstation for model execution
decisions.detail: A workstation will be deployed on-site. Images will be sent to the client's Access Point (AP), which will then trigger the workstation to run the model.

evidence.quote: A workstation will be set up on-site; images are sent to the client's AP, which then calls the workstation to run the model

decisions.title: GIS Provider B via GeoServer for base map
decisions.detail: The base map will be supplied by GIS Provider B using GeoServer.

evidence.quote: The base map will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer

decisions.title: Map viewer features: load history, filter, display points
decisions.detail: The map viewer will be capable of loading historically uploaded imagery, offering filtering options by date and image-name keyword (e.g., satellite, aerial photo, drone), and displaying points on the map.

evidence.quote: The map viewer can load historically uploaded imagery, supports filtering the list by date and image-name keyword (type: satellite / aerial photo / drone), and displays points on the map

decisions.title: Map zoom to point feature
decisions.detail: When a point is opened, a 'locate' button will be available, which, when pressed, will automatically zoom the map to that specific location.

evidence.quote: Opening a point shows a "locate" button; the map automatically zooms to that location

decisions.title: Automatic imagery revert to point display on zoom out
decisions.detail: If the map is zoomed out beyond a predetermined scale, the imagery will automatically revert to displaying points instead of the full imagery.

evidence.quote: When the map is zoomed out past a certain scale, imagery automatically reverts to point display

decisions.title: Map view lock in comparison mode
decisions.detail: In comparison mode, the map view will be locked, preventing panning. A 'release drag mode' button will be provided to re-enable panning.

evidence.quote: In comparison mode, the map view is locked and cannot be panned; a "release drag mode" button is provided to restore panning

decisions.title: Adjustable image transparency for map viewer
decisions.detail: Users will be able to adjust image transparency, which is noted as an important feature for orthophoto analysis.

evidence.quote: Users can adjust image transparency (an important feature for orthophoto analysis)

decisions.title: Map viewer design references existing system
decisions.detail: The design of the map viewer will reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System.

evidence.quote: The map viewer's design references the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System

decisions.title: Project organization by year for suspected points
decisions.detail: Projects will be organized by year, and suspected points can be created within these year-based projects.

evidence.quote: Projects are organized by year; suspected points can be created within a project

decisions.title: Confirmation needed for splitting suspected point layers by county/city
decisions.detail: A decision on whether to split suspected point layers by county/city is pending confirmation in the requirements interview.

evidence.quote: Whether to split layers by county/city is pending confirmation in the requirements interview

decisions.title: Suspected points require polygon upload, no image upload
decisions.detail: Suspected points will require an uploaded polygon for definition, but no image upload is necessary for them.

evidence.quote: Suspected points require an uploaded polygon; no image upload is needed

decisions.title: Each year forms one suspected point layer
decisions.detail: For suspected points, each year will constitute a single layer.

evidence.quote: Each year forms one layer

decisions.title: New Survey Point Data interface creation
decisions.detail: A new interface for 'New Survey Point Data' must be added. This interface needs to include fields for drawing number and key-monitoring-zone, referencing manual figure 2.13.

evidence.quote: Need to add a "New Survey Point Data" interface (including drawing number and key-monitoring-zone fields, refer to manual figure 2.13)

decisions.title: Required fields for New Survey Point Data interface
decisions.detail: The 'New Survey Point Data' form, which supports before/after edit states, must include fields for survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, and approximate location.

evidence.quote: [Screenshot: "New Survey Point Data" form, before/after clicking "Edit" — fields include survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, approximate location]

decisions.title: Migration of legacy MS file management with redesign
decisions.detail: The existing MS file management system will be migrated to the new platform. Its interface will be redesigned, but the underlying functionality will remain unchanged.

evidence.quote: The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system; the interface will be redesigned but functionality will remain unchanged

decisions.title: Security compliance drives MIS migration
decisions.detail: The primary motivation for migrating the legacy MIS system is to meet information-security compliance requirements.

evidence.quote: The main reason for migration is information-security compliance requirements

decisions.title: MIS migration details pending confirmation
decisions.detail: Specific details regarding the legacy MIS system migration are awaiting confirmation during the requirements interview.

evidence.quote: Migration details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview

decisions.title: Legacy system had email-based follow-up/reminder
decisions.detail: The legacy system includes a follow-up/reminder feature that primarily uses email notifications.

evidence.quote: The legacy system already has a follow-up/reminder feature, primarily via email notification

decisions.title: Follow-up items selectable from suspected points
decisions.detail: For the new system's follow-up feature, items to be followed up on can be selected from the list of suspected points and integrated into the follow-up module.

evidence.quote: Items to follow up on can be selected from suspected points and integrated into the follow-up module

decisions.title: Schedule-tracking interface for follow-up
decisions.detail: The new system's follow-up feature will include a schedule-tracking interface, which could display information like days remaining.

evidence.quote: Add a schedule-tracking interface (e.g. showing days remaining)

decisions.title: Follow-up dispatch interface to be developed
decisions.detail: A follow-up dispatch interface is planned for the new system, with Shao-Chien already possessing a first draft.

evidence.quote: Follow-up dispatch interface (Shao-Chien already has a first draft)

decisions.title: Follow-up connects to survey-report management
decisions.detail: After a follow-up action, the system will connect to survey-report management, allowing for the recording of time, description, action taken, and uploaded images.

evidence.quote: After follow-up, this connects to survey-report management: including time, description, action taken, and uploaded images

decisions.title: Data flow document for AI interpretation results
decisions.detail: A-Bao has requested a data-flow document explaining the movement of data after AI interpretation completes, specifically addressing data source, and computation location.

evidence.quote: A-Bao needs a data-flow document explaining how data moves after AI interpretation completes — where the data is pulled from, and where computation happens
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: [Data Flow Documentation (requested by A-Bao)]

decisions.title: Defined data flow for AI interpretation
decisions.detail: The data flow for AI interpretation encompasses: image source -> interpretation task execution (workstation) -> result output -> monitoring database.

evidence.quote: Data flow covers: image source → interpretation task execution (workstation) → result output → monitoring database
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: [Data Flow Documentation (requested by A-Bao)]

decisions.title: Project kick-off expected mid-May if bidding successful
decisions.detail: The project is currently in the bidding process, with an anticipated kick-off in mid-May if the bidding proceeds smoothly.

evidence.quote: The project is currently in the bidding process; if things go smoothly, it's expected to kick off in mid-May

decisions.title: Immediate requirements interview post-bid award
decisions.detail: On the afternoon of the day the bid is awarded, a requirements interview and interface confirmation may be required immediately due to tight timelines.

evidence.quote: On the afternoon of the day the bid is awarded, a requirements interview and interface confirmation may need to happen immediately

decisions.title: Sherry to finish 'add suspected point' data interface by 5/18
decisions.detail: Sherry is tasked with completing the 'add suspected point' data interface, referencing figure 2.13, by May 18th.

evidence.quote: Sherry | Finish the "add suspected point" data interface (refer to figure 2.13) | Before 5/18

decisions.title: Sherry to organize interfaces for requirements interview by 5/20
decisions.detail: Sherry is responsible for organizing existing interfaces, including those owned by Shao-Chien, for use in the requirements interview by May 20th.

evidence.quote: Sherry | Organize existing interfaces (including the parts Shao-Chien owns) for use in the requirements interview | Before 5/20

decisions.title: Sherry to prepare data-flow document for A-Bao by 5/18
decisions.detail: Sherry is assigned to prepare the data-flow document, covering image source, compute node, and data direction, for A-Bao's review by May 18th.

evidence.quote: Sherry | Prepare the data-flow document (image source, compute node, data direction) for A-Bao to review | Before 5/18

decisions.title: Next meeting scheduled for 2026-05-15
decisions.detail: The next meeting is scheduled for May 15, 2026.

decisions.title: Next meeting topic: system architecture
decisions.detail: The planned topic for the next meeting is to discuss system architecture.

decisions.detail: The first requirements interview is scheduled for May 20, 2026.

decisions.detail: The planned topic for the requirements interview is the 'First requirements interview'.

evidence.quote: Planned topic: First requirements interview

decisions.detail: Preparation for the requirements interview includes consolidating existing interfaces, preparing a system architecture diagram, and a data flow diagram.

evidence.quote: Prep needed: Consolidate existing interfaces, system architecture diagram, data flow diagram

decisions.detail: Details regarding the notification mechanism for AI interpretation task completion are an open item needing confirmation.

decisions.detail: It is an open question whether the suspected-point layer should be segmented by county/city.

decisions.detail: The interface design details for the MIS migration are an open item requiring confirmation.

decisions.detail: Who will receive follow-up notifications and according to what schedule is an open question that needs to be confirmed.

implementation_scope: Develop AI interpretation background task system with completion notification similar to CC platform (D001).
Implement image ingestion module supporting two sources: Vendor A batch and individual user uploads (D003).
Build UI/backend for user confirmation (yes/no) and monitoring decision for suspected points (D004).
Implement polygon upload for monitoring points, including storage for reason and validation for polygon area (D005).
Develop a duplicate image upload prevention mechanism for large image files (D006).
Implement manual annotation functionality for two-period image comparison (D007).
Develop map viewer features: load historical imagery, filter by date and image-name keyword (satellite, aerial photo, drone), display points (D010).
Implement 'locate' button functionality within the map viewer to zoom to a specific point (D011).
Develop logic for automatic map imagery reversion to point display when zoomed out past a predetermined scale (D012).
Implement map view locking in comparison mode with a 'release drag mode' button (D013).
Integrate adjustable image transparency controls into the map viewer (D014).
Implement project organization by year for suspected points, allowing creation within these projects (D016).
Develop suspected point definition requiring polygon upload (no image upload) (D018).
Implement suspected point layering where each year forms a single layer (D019).

Create a 'New Survey Point Data' interface/form with fields for drawing number, key-monitoring-zone, survey serial number, village, monitoring date, monitoring method, volume, area, section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, and approximate location, supporting before/after edit states (D020, D021).
Migrate the legacy MS file management system to the new platform, redesigning the interface while maintaining existing underlying functionality (D022).
Develop a follow-up module allowing selection of items from suspected points for follow-up (D026).
Implement a schedule-tracking interface within the follow-up feature (e.g., displaying days remaining) (D027).
Develop a follow-up dispatch interface (utilizing Shao-Chien's draft) (D028).
Integrate follow-up actions with survey-report management to record time, description, action taken, and uploaded images (D029).

constraints.text: An on-site workstation must be deployed for AI model execution; images are sent to the client's Access Point (AP), which triggers the workstation to run the model (D008, D031).
The base map must be supplied by GIS Provider B using GeoServer (D009).
The map viewer's design must reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System (D015).
The legacy system had an email-based follow-up/reminder feature, indicating an existing expectation for such functionality (D025).
Project kick-off is anticipated in mid-May if bidding is successful (D032).
Immediate requirements interview and interface confirmation may be required post-bid award due to tight timelines (D033).
The 'add suspected point' data interface (referencing figure 2.13) must be completed by May 18th (D034).
Existing interfaces (including Shao-Chien's parts) must be organized for the requirements interview by May 20th (D035).
A data-flow document (covering image source, compute node, and data direction) must be prepared for A-Bao by May 18th (D036).
Preparation for the requirements interview requires consolidating existing interfaces, a system architecture diagram, and a data flow diagram (D041).

architectural_decisions.text: The two-period image comparison feature will no longer source images from the image library; manual user annotation will be used instead (D007).
The existing MS file management system will be migrated to the new platform, with a redesigned interface but unchanged underlying functionality (D022).

open_technical_questions: What are the specific details of the notification mechanism for AI interpretation task completion? (D002, D042)
Should suspected point layers be split by county/city? This impacts data modeling and GIS layer management. (D017, D043)
What are the interface design details for the MIS migration? (D024, D044)

acceptance_criteria: Given an AI interpretation run is initiated, Then a background task related to this run appears in the task list.
Given an AI interpretation run completes successfully, Then a completion notification is sent to the user.
Given an AI interpretation run is initiated with a batch of images from Vendor A, Then the system successfully processes these images for interpretation.
Given an AI interpretation run is initiated with individually uploaded user images, Then the system successfully processes these images for interpretation.
Given a list of suspected points is generated after AI interpretation, When the user views the list, Then each point presents an option to 'Confirm' (Yes/No).
Given a suspected point is confirmed, Then the user is able to decide whether to place it under 'Watch' or 'Monitoring'.
Given a user chooses to place a suspected point under monitoring, When the user proceeds, Then a reason must be provided.
Given a user chooses to place a suspected point under monitoring, When the user proceeds, Then a corresponding polygon must be uploaded.
Given a large-area polygon is uploaded for monitoring, Then the system accepts it.
Given an overly small-area polygon is uploaded for monitoring, Then the system automatically filters it out and prevents monitoring setup.
Given an image is uploaded, When an attempt is made to upload the exact same image again, Then the system prevents the duplicate upload.
Given the two-period image comparison feature is used, Then users are able to manually annotate images for comparison.
Given the two-period image comparison feature is used, Then the system does not source images from the image library.
Given the map viewer is loaded, Then a base map is displayed.
Given the map viewer is opened, Then historically uploaded imagery is displayed.
Given historically uploaded imagery is displayed, When the user applies a date filter, Then only imagery matching the filter criteria is shown.
Given historically uploaded imagery is displayed, When the user applies an image-name keyword filter (e.g., 'satellite'), Then only imagery matching the filter criteria is shown.
Given there are points associated with imagery, Then these points are displayed on the map.
Given a point is opened on the map, Then a 'locate' button is visible.
When the 'locate' button is pressed for an opened point, Then the map automatically zooms to the specific location of that point.
Given the map displays full imagery, When the map is zoomed out beyond a predetermined scale, Then the imagery automatically reverts to displaying points.
Given the map is in comparison mode, Then panning the map is prevented.
Given the map is in comparison mode, Then a 'release drag mode' button is provided.
When the 'release drag mode' button is pressed in comparison mode, Then panning the map is re-enabled.
Given an image is displayed in the map viewer, Then the user can adjust its transparency level.
When the image transparency is adjusted, Then the visual transparency of the image changes accordingly on the map.
Given a new project is created, Then it is organized by year.
Given a year-based project exists, Then suspected points can be created within it.
Given a new suspected point is created, Then a polygon must be uploaded for its definition.
Given a new suspected point is created, When an attempt is made to upload an image for it, Then the system prevents the image upload.
Given suspected points are created for a specific year, Then all points from that year belong to a single layer.
Given the system is accessed, Then a 'New Survey Point Data' interface is available.
Given the 'New Survey Point Data' interface is accessed, Then it includes fields for 'drawing number' and 'key-monitoring-zone'.

Given the 'New Survey Point Data' form is viewed in its initial state, Then it displays fields for 'survey serial number', 'drawing number', 'key monitoring zone', 'village', 'monitoring date', 'monitoring method', 'volume (cubic meters)', 'area (square meters)', 'section', 'lot number', 'X/Y coordinates', 'longitude/latitude', and 'approximate location'.
Given the 'New Survey Point Data' form is in an editable state, Then all specified fields remain visible and editable.
Given 'monitoring method' is selected, Then it allows selection from 'precise coordinates', 'aerial survey', 'rapid monitoring', 'unmanned equipment'.
Given the MS file management system is accessed on the new platform, Then its interface reflects the redesign.
Given the MS file management system is accessed on the new platform, Then its core functionality (e.g., uploading, downloading, organizing files) remains consistent with the legacy system.
Given a list of suspected points is available, Then individual or multiple suspected points can be selected for follow-up.
When suspected points are selected for follow-up, Then they are successfully integrated into the follow-up module.
Given the follow-up module is accessed, Then a schedule-tracking interface is visible.
Given a follow-up item has a defined schedule, Then the schedule-tracking interface displays relevant schedule information (e.g., 'days remaining').
Given the follow-up module is accessed, Then a follow-up dispatch interface is available.
Given a follow-up action is completed, Then the system provides an option to connect to survey-report management.
When connected to survey-report management after a follow-up, Then fields are available to record 'time', 'description', 'action taken', and 'uploaded images'.
Given an AI interpretation task completes, Then the results are stored in the monitoring database.
test_scenarios: Initiate an AI interpretation run and verify a background task is created and visible in the task list.
Verify that a notification is received upon the successful completion of an AI interpretation task.
Initiate AI interpretation using a large-scale nationwide batch image source from Vendor A.
Initiate AI interpretation using individually uploaded user images.
After AI interpretation, confirm a suspected point and place it under monitoring, ensuring reason and polygon are provided.
After AI interpretation, confirm a suspected point and place it under watch.
After AI interpretation, reject a suspected point (mark as No) and verify it is not placed under watch/monitoring.
Attempt to place a suspected point under monitoring without providing a reason, verify an error message appears.
Attempt to place a suspected point under monitoring without uploading a polygon, verify an error message appears.
Upload an image. Attempt to upload the exact same image again and verify the system prevents the duplicate upload with an appropriate message.
Use the two-period image comparison feature and verify that manual annotation tools are available and functional.
Attempt to source images from the image library for two-period comparison and verify it is not possible.
Load the map viewer and verify historical imagery is visible, along with any associated points.
Apply a date filter (e.g., "last month") in the map viewer and verify only relevant imagery appears.
Apply an image-name keyword filter (e.g., "drone") in the map viewer and verify only relevant imagery appears.
Open a point on the map, click the "locate" button, and verify the map automatically zooms to that point's location.
Zoom into the map to display full imagery. Gradually zoom out and verify that at a specific scale, the map view automatically changes from imagery to displaying points.
Enter comparison mode and attempt to pan the map; verify it is locked. Click "release drag mode" and verify panning is re-enabled.
Load an orthophoto in the map viewer. Adjust the transparency slider/control and visually confirm the image transparency changes.
Create a new project and verify it is correctly organized by the current year.
Create multiple suspected points for the same year and verify they appear on the same map layer.
Create suspected points for different years and verify they appear on distinct map layers.
Successfully create a suspected point by uploading a valid polygon for its definition.
Attempt to create a suspected point without uploading a polygon and verify an error message is displayed.
Navigate to and open the "New Survey Point Data" interface. Verify the presence and functionality of 'drawing number' and 'key-monitoring-zone' fields.
Open the "New Survey Point Data" form, visually confirm all specified fields are present, and verify the 'before/after edit states' functionality.
For the 'monitoring method' field, select each available option ('precise coordinates', 'aerial survey', 'rapid monitoring', 'unmanned equipment') and verify it is accepted.
Perform typical file management operations (upload, download, rename, delete) in the new MS file management system and verify the functionality matches the legacy system.
Select a single suspected point from the list and integrate it into the follow-up module.
Select multiple suspected points from the list and integrate them into the follow-up module.
Open the follow-up module and verify a schedule-tracking interface is present and displays 'days remaining' (or similar) for an item with a set schedule.
Navigate to the follow-up module and verify the presence of the follow-up dispatch interface.
Complete a follow-up action and verify the option to connect to survey-report management is available and functional.
In survey-report management, confirm the fields for 'time', 'description', 'action taken', and 'uploaded images' are present, functional, and store data correctly after a follow-up action.
After an AI interpretation run, verify that the interpretation results are persisted and retrievable from the monitoring database.
edge_cases: What happens if the AI interpretation task fails? Is a failure notification sent?
What happens if an AI interpretation task is canceled? Is a cancellation notification sent?
What is the definition of an "overly small area" polygon? How is it filtered out (e.g., error message, silent discard, UI prevention)?
What are the character limits or validation rules for the 'reason' field when placing a point under monitoring?
What happens if the polygon upload for monitoring fails (e.g., network error, invalid file type)?
What defines a "duplicate" image? (e.g., filename only, content hash, metadata)?
Test uploading images with the same content but different filenames to verify duplicate detection.
Test uploading images with slightly different metadata but the same visual content for duplicate detection.
What happens if no historical imagery exists in the map viewer?
What happens if a filter (date or keyword) yields no results in the map viewer?
Test various keyword combinations (e.g., "satellite" AND "2023") for filtering imagery.
What happens if a point's coordinates are invalid or missing when using the 'locate' button?
How does the 'locate' button behave if multiple points are selected or opened simultaneously?
What is the exact predetermined zoom scale at which imagery automatically reverts to point display?
How does the system handle map areas with no points but with imagery when zoomed out past the threshold?
What are the minimum and maximum transparency values for image adjustment (e.g., 0% to 100%)?
Does the image transparency setting persist across user sessions or for different images?
What are the validation rules for the polygon uploaded for suspected points (e.g., minimum vertices, self-intersection, area limits)?
What are the validation rules for each field in the 'New Survey Point Data' form (e.g., date format, coordinate ranges, numeric limits for volume/area)?
What happens if required fields in the 'New Survey Point Data' form are left blank upon submission?
unresolved_behaviors: Details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism (e.g., in-app, email, push, content).
Whether suspected point layers should be split by county/city.
Interface design details for the MIS migration (how the redesigned interface will look and behave while maintaining functionality).
Who receives follow-up notifications and on what schedule.

decision_history: Each AI interpretation run will create a background task within the task list, with a notification sent upon its completion (D001).
Imagery for AI interpretation will originate from two sources: a one-time nationwide batch from Vendor A, or individual user uploads (D003).
Upon completion of AI interpretation and generation of suspected points, users can confirm/reject points and decide whether to place them under watch or monitoring (D004).
Placing a suspected point under monitoring requires users to provide a reason and upload a polygon, with large-area polygons accepted and small ones filtered (D005).
A duplicate-prevention mechanism is required for image uploads due to large file sizes (D006).
The two-period image comparison feature will shift from pulling images from the image library to manual user annotation (D007).
A workstation will be deployed on-site; images sent to the client's Access Point will trigger the workstation to run the AI model (D008).
The base map will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer (D009).
The map viewer will support loading historical imagery, filtering by date and image type keywords, and displaying points (D010).
A 'locate' button in the map viewer will automatically zoom to the point's location (D011).
The map viewer will automatically revert to point display when zoomed out past a certain scale (D012).
In comparison mode, the map view will be locked for panning, with a 'release drag mode' button to re-enable it (D013).
Users will be able to adjust image transparency in the map viewer for orthophoto analysis (D014).
Projects will be organized by year, allowing suspected points to be created within these year-based projects (D016).
Suspected points require an uploaded polygon for definition, but no image upload is needed (D018).
Each year will constitute a single layer for suspected points (D019).
A new 'New Survey Point Data' interface must be added, including fields like drawing number and key-monitoring-zone, referencing manual figure 2.13 (D020).

The 'New Survey Point Data' form requires fields for survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method, volume, area, section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, and approximate location (D021).
The existing MS file management system will be migrated to the new platform with a redesigned interface, while core functionality remains unchanged (D022).
The migration of the legacy MIS system is primarily driven by information-security compliance requirements (D023).
Follow-up items can be selected from suspected points and integrated into the follow-up module (D026).
The follow-up feature will include a schedule-tracking interface showing information like days remaining (D027).
A follow-up dispatch interface is planned, with a first draft already prepared by Shao-Chien (D028).
After a follow-up action, the system will connect to survey-report management to record time, description, action, and uploaded images (D029).
A data-flow document for AI interpretation results, covering data source and computation location, has been requested by A-Bao (D030).
The data flow for AI interpretation is defined as: image source -> interpretation task execution (workstation) -> result output -> monitoring database (D031).
dependencies: External imagery from Vendor A is required for the one-time large-scale nationwide batch (D003).
On-site workstation deployment and integration with the client's Access Point are required for AI model execution (D008).
GIS Provider B and GeoServer are required to supply the base map (D009).
The map viewer design must reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System (D015).
Preparation for the requirements interview is dependent on consolidating existing interfaces, preparing a system architecture diagram, and a data flow diagram (D041).
The follow-up dispatch interface development relies on Shao-Chien's existing first draft (D028).
Engineering team to implement a duplicate image prevention mechanism for uploads (inferred from D006).
System Analyst/Engineering team to prepare the data-flow document for A-Bao's review (D030, D036).
risks: Project kick-off is contingent on successful bidding; any delay in the bidding process will directly impact the project's aggressive timeline (D032).

Tight timelines require an immediate requirements interview and interface confirmation post-bid award (D033), posing a risk of insufficient stakeholder availability or hurried decision-making, potentially leading to rework or missed requirements.
alignment_gaps: Details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism (D002, D042) need to be confirmed, as different stakeholders may have varying expectations for notification content, frequency, and delivery methods.

A decision on whether to split suspected point layers by county/city (D017, D043) is pending, which is critical for data organization and user experience and could lead to differing preferences among stakeholder groups.

Specific interface design details for the MIS migration (D024, D044) are pending confirmation; while functionality remains, unaligned design expectations could lead to scope changes or user dissatisfaction.
Who will receive follow-up notifications and on what schedule (D045) needs to be clarified, as different roles/departments may have specific requirements for timing and recipients.
open_questions: What are the specific details of the notification mechanism for AI interpretation task completion? (D002, D042)
Should suspected point layers be split by county/city? (D017, D043)
What are the specific interface design details for the legacy MIS system migration? (D024, D044)
Who will receive follow-up notifications and according to what schedule? (D045)
milestones: Project kick-off expected mid-May 2026 if bidding successful (D032).
Requirements interview and interface confirmation may be required immediately on the afternoon of the bid award (D033).
Sherry to finish the 'add suspected point' data interface, referencing figure 2.13, by 2026-05-18 (D034).
Sherry to prepare the data-flow document for A-Bao's review, covering image source, compute node, and data direction, by 2026-05-18 (D036).
Next meeting scheduled for 2026-05-15, with system architecture as the planned topic (D037, D038).
Sherry to organize existing interfaces (including Shao-Chien's parts) for use in the requirements interview by 2026-05-20 (D035).

system_requirements: The system SHALL create a background task for each AI interpretation run within the task list.
The system SHALL send a notification upon the completion of an AI interpretation task.
The system SHALL accept imagery for AI interpretation from a one-time, large-scale nationwide batch provided by Vendor A.
The system SHALL accept individual images uploaded by the user for AI interpretation.
Upon completion of AI interpretation and generation of a list of suspected points, the system SHALL allow users to confirm or reject individual suspected points.
The system SHALL allow users to decide whether to place confirmed suspected points under watch or monitoring.
When a suspected point is placed under monitoring, the system SHALL require the user to provide a reason.
When a suspected point is placed under monitoring, the system SHALL require the user to upload a corresponding polygon.
The system SHALL filter out overly small polygons provided for monitoring.
The system MUST prevent duplicate image uploads.
The map viewer SHALL be capable of loading historically uploaded imagery.
The map viewer SHALL provide filtering options for historically uploaded imagery by date.
The map viewer SHALL provide filtering options for historically uploaded imagery by image-name keyword (e.g., satellite, aerial photo, drone).
The map viewer SHALL display suspected points on the map.
When a point is opened in the map viewer, the system SHALL display a 'locate' button.
Upon user activation of the 'locate' button, the map viewer SHALL automatically zoom to the location of the selected point.
The map viewer SHALL automatically revert from displaying full imagery to displaying points when zoomed out beyond a predetermined scale.
In comparison mode, the map viewer SHALL lock the map view, preventing panning.
The map viewer SHALL provide a 'release drag mode' button to re-enable panning in comparison mode.
The map viewer SHALL allow users to adjust image transparency.
The system SHALL organize projects by year.
The system SHALL allow creation of suspected points within year-based projects.
The system SHALL require an uploaded polygon for the definition of suspected points.
The system MUST NOT require an image upload for suspected points.
The system SHALL represent each year's suspected points as a single layer.
The system SHALL provide a 'New Survey Point Data' interface.
The 'New Survey Point Data' interface SHALL include fields for 'drawing number' and 'key-monitoring-zone' (referencing manual figure 2.13).
The follow-up module SHALL allow users to select items for follow-up from the list of suspected points.
The follow-up module SHALL include a schedule-tracking interface capable of displaying information such as days remaining.
The system SHALL provide a follow-up dispatch interface.
After a follow-up action, the system SHALL connect to survey-report management to record follow-up details including time, description, action taken, and uploaded images.
data_flows: Images -> Client's Access Point (AP) -> On-site Workstation (trigger model execution).
Image Source -> AI Interpretation Task Execution (on-site workstation) -> Result Output -> Monitoring Database.
Follow-up action completion -> Survey Report Management (record time, description, action taken, uploaded images).
interface_contracts: Define 'New Survey Point Data' interface including fields: drawing number, key-monitoring-zone.

The 'New Survey Point Data' form SHALL include the following fields: survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, and approximate location. The form SHALL support 'before edit' and 'after edit' states.

constraints.text: AI interpretation model execution MUST occur on an on-site workstation, triggered by the client's Access Point (AP) upon receiving images.
The system SHALL use GIS Provider B via GeoServer to supply the base map.
The design of the map viewer SHALL reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System.
The migration of the legacy MIS system MUST meet information-security compliance requirements.
The data flow for AI interpretation SHALL follow the sequence: image source -> interpretation task execution (workstation) -> result output -> monitoring database.

architectural_decisions.text: The two-period image comparison feature SHALL rely on manual user annotation instead of sourcing images from an image library.
The existing MS file management system functionality SHALL be migrated to the new platform with a redesigned interface, maintaining existing underlying functionality.

open_analysis_questions: What are the specific details of the notification mechanism for AI interpretation task completion (e.g., channels, content, recipients)?
Should suspected point layers be segmented by county/city?
What are the specific interface design details for the legacy MIS system migration?
Who will receive follow-up notifications and on what schedule?

stakeholder.business_summary: This meeting outlined the key features for our new system, including how Artificial Intelligence will help identify potential issues from imagery, significant improvements to our mapping tools, and an enhanced process for tracking follow-up actions. A critical component is the migration of our existing file management system to ensure we meet security compliance standards. The project is currently in the bidding phase, with an expected mid-May kick-off and an immediate need for detailed requirements discussions upon bid award.
key_impact: Significantly improves efficiency by automating the initial identification of potential issues using AI.
Enhances decision-making and user experience with advanced mapping features, including historical data review and adjustable imagery.
Ensures data integrity and operational efficiency by preventing duplicate image uploads and providing structured input for survey data.
Addresses critical information security and compliance requirements by migrating the legacy file management system.
Streamlines workflow and accountability with new features for tracking, dispatching, and reporting on follow-up actions.
decisions_needing_alignment: Confirm the specific details of how users will be notified when AI analysis tasks are completed.
Decide whether suspected point layers should be organized and displayed separately for each county or city.
Finalize the specific interface design details for the migration of the legacy file management system.
Clarify who should receive follow-up notifications and according to what schedule.
major_unresolved_questions: What are the final design and functional specifics for migrating our legacy file management system, especially concerning user experience and ensuring security compliance?
Should the system automatically categorize and display potential issues (suspected points) by individual county or city, or will a broader view be sufficient for our operational needs?
How exactly will users be notified when the AI analysis for new images is completed, and what level of detail will these notifications provide?

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs accurately reflect the extracted decisions. No hallucinations, inconsistencies, or significant quality issues were found. Open questions are consistently identified across relevant views. No prompt injection or sensitive content detected.

source_map.decision_title: AI interpretation run creates background task with notification
evidence_quotes: Each AI interpretation run creates one task, which runs in the background in the task list (similar to how the CC platform operates); a notification is sent when it completes

source_map.decision_title: Notification mechanism details for AI interpretation tasks are pending confirmation
evidence_quotes: Notification mechanism details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview

source_map.decision_title: Two image sources for AI interpretation
evidence_quotes: Two image sources: a one-time large-scale nationwide batch of imagery obtained from Vendor A, or individual images uploaded by the user

source_map.decision_title: User confirmation and monitoring decision for suspected points
evidence_quotes: After interpretation completes, a list is generated; the user can confirm suspected points (yes/no) and decide whether to place them under watch/monitoring

source_map.decision_title: Monitoring requires reason and polygon upload
evidence_quotes: When placing a point under monitoring, the reason must be filled in and a polygon uploaded (large-area polygons are valid; overly small areas are automatically filtered out)

source_map.decision_title: Duplicate image upload prevention
evidence_quotes: Images must not be uploaded more than once (image files are large, so a duplicate-prevention mechanism is needed)

source_map.decision_title: Manual annotation for two-period image comparison
evidence_quotes: Two-period image comparison will no longer pull from the image library — the user will annotate this manually instead

source_map.decision_title: On-site workstation for model execution
evidence_quotes: A workstation will be set up on-site; images are sent to the client's AP, which then calls the workstation to run the model

source_map.decision_title: GIS Provider B via GeoServer for base map
evidence_quotes: The base map will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer

source_map.decision_title: Map viewer features: load history, filter, display points
evidence_quotes: The map viewer can load historically uploaded imagery, supports filtering the list by date and image-name keyword (type: satellite / aerial photo / drone), and displays points on the map

source_map.decision_title: Map zoom to point feature
evidence_quotes: Opening a point shows a "locate" button; the map automatically zooms to that location

source_map.decision_title: Automatic imagery revert to point display on zoom out
evidence_quotes: When the map is zoomed out past a certain scale, imagery automatically reverts to point display

source_map.decision_title: Map view lock in comparison mode
evidence_quotes: In comparison mode, the map view is locked and cannot be panned; a "release drag mode" button is provided to restore panning

source_map.decision_title: Adjustable image transparency for map viewer
evidence_quotes: Users can adjust image transparency (an important feature for orthophoto analysis)

source_map.decision_title: Map viewer design references existing system
evidence_quotes: The map viewer's design references the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System

source_map.decision_title: Project organization by year for suspected points
evidence_quotes: Projects are organized by year; suspected points can be created within a project

source_map.decision_title: Confirmation needed for splitting suspected point layers by county/city
evidence_quotes: Whether to split layers by county/city is pending confirmation in the requirements interview

source_map.decision_title: Suspected points require polygon upload, no image upload
evidence_quotes: Suspected points require an uploaded polygon; no image upload is needed

source_map.decision_title: Each year forms one suspected point layer
evidence_quotes: Each year forms one layer

source_map.decision_title: New Survey Point Data interface creation
evidence_quotes: Need to add a "New Survey Point Data" interface (including drawing number and key-monitoring-zone fields, refer to manual figure 2.13)

source_map.decision_title: Required fields for New Survey Point Data interface
evidence_quotes: [Screenshot: "New Survey Point Data" form, before/after clicking "Edit" — fields include survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, approximate location]

source_map.decision_title: Migration of legacy MS file management with redesign
evidence_quotes: The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system; the interface will be redesigned but functionality will remain unchanged

source_map.decision_title: Security compliance drives MIS migration
evidence_quotes: The main reason for migration is information-security compliance requirements

source_map.decision_title: MIS migration details pending confirmation
evidence_quotes: Migration details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview

source_map.decision_title: Legacy system had email-based follow-up/reminder
evidence_quotes: The legacy system already has a follow-up/reminder feature, primarily via email notification

source_map.decision_title: Follow-up items selectable from suspected points
evidence_quotes: Items to follow up on can be selected from suspected points and integrated into the follow-up module

source_map.decision_title: Schedule-tracking interface for follow-up
evidence_quotes: Add a schedule-tracking interface (e.g. showing days remaining)

source_map.decision_title: Follow-up dispatch interface to be developed
evidence_quotes: Follow-up dispatch interface (Shao-Chien already has a first draft)

source_map.decision_title: Follow-up connects to survey-report management
evidence_quotes: After follow-up, this connects to survey-report management: including time, description, action taken, and uploaded images

source_map.decision_title: Data flow document for AI interpretation results
evidence_quotes: A-Bao needs a data-flow document explaining how data moves after AI interpretation completes — where the data is pulled from, and where computation happens

source_map.decision_title: Defined data flow for AI interpretation
evidence_quotes: Data flow covers: image source → interpretation task execution (workstation) → result output → monitoring database

source_map.decision_title: Project kick-off expected mid-May if bidding successful
evidence_quotes: The project is currently in the bidding process; if things go smoothly, it's expected to kick off in mid-May

source_map.decision_title: Immediate requirements interview post-bid award
evidence_quotes: On the afternoon of the day the bid is awarded, a requirements interview and interface confirmation may need to happen immediately
